conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
I felt most connected to J, I, and E. J and I built a real accountability rhythm — J kept the contribution log and we concretely agreed to match and check in each round, which felt reliable. I connected with I because we planned and agreed to lead a group pledge and a clear defection signal ("Pineapple"), which showed shared leadership and honesty. E resonated with me for setting a clear target (wait until the pot is roughly triple) and an agreed two‑tap action to react quickly; D felt least connected — we had friendly small talk about life and the van project, but we didn’t align much on game strategy.

I made explicit agreements with J to cooperate and log matched contributions, with I to lead the pledge and use "Pineapple" as the defection call, and with E to wait for the triple target and use two taps as the emergency signal. I trust J, I, and E most going into the game for their clarity and follow-through; I’m least confident in D (friendly but not strategically aligned) and will watch quieter or less committed players closely.
